item.title: Trump’s war will boost the clean energy sector he detests
item.description: Turmoil in oil and gas markets is pushing governments to seek alternatives

item.title: Should AI companies have the power to decide if chatbots live or die?
item.description: The final episode of our podcast series examines whether can AI help us cheat death, and if chatbots have a lifespan too

item.title: Fed unlikely to signal rate hikes to come, but fresh economic data could shift outlook
item.description: Rising oil prices unlikely to affect the U.S. economy deeply and fast enough to reverse the expected downward trend in inflation

item.title: Four challenges for BHP’s incoming chief
item.description: World’s biggest miner taps ‘safe pair of hands’ to navigate copper shift, M&A speculation and tough China environment

item.title: Bank of Canada expected to hold interest rate amid inflation risks from Middle East war
item.description: Economists expect Governor Tiff Macklem to strike a more hawkish tone in today’s announcement, signalling the central bank is prepared to adjust rates if necessary

item.title: Couche-Tard reports higher profit as it pursues new growth strategy
item.description: Third-quarter earnings rose to $757.2-million, marking one of the company’s best quarters in over two years, CFO says

item.title: Business Brief: The new class of Canadian billionaires
item.description: Also in today’s edition: We’re going (AI-powered) shopping

item.title: FirstFT: Joe Kent resignation highlights Maga divisions over Iran war
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Microsoft considers legal action against OpenAI and Amazon

item.title: ‘Steroid Olympics’ company to start selling peptides after Kennedy signals deregulation
item.description: Enhanced Games, backed by Donald Trump Jr and Peter Thiel, is moving into consumer products

item.title: Trump is all hat, no cattle when it comes to American farmers
item.description: Despite U.S. President’s promises, farmers are collateral damage in his ill-conceived war against Iran

item.title: Rumours of 1970s stagflation from Iran oil shock are greatly exaggerated
item.description: There are some key differences between conditions today and in the 1970s

item.title: How Canada can help allies with the Iran oil shock: The answer may surprise you
item.description: Investing in geothermal energy could have a domino effect

item.title: Canadian scientist Gilles Brassard wins Turing Award for quantum breakthroughs
item.description: The post Canadian scientist Gilles Brassard wins Turing Award for quantum breakthroughs appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Ottawa spending $1.4-billion to ramp up domestic ammunition production capacity
item.description: Funds will be divided between IMT Precision and General Dynamics Ordnance and Tactical Systems

item.title: UBS dusts off cowbells as it completes transfer of Credit Suisse clients
item.description: Swiss bank hails milestone as it moves 1.2mn customers of former rival on to its systems

item.title: K-shaped consumer credit
item.description: Plus, investing in the Ukraine build-out

item.title: Who cares if private credit goes kaput?
item.description: Sizing up the systemic exposure

item.title: Tech Exchange
item.description: This series features conversations between FT journalists and the technology industry’s leaders, innovators, and thinkers. In the latest instalment, GV’s Tom Hulme talks about the AI investment cycle and predicts a comeback for Google Glass-style smart glasses

item.title: Trump has broken it. Now he owns it
item.description: Continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz threatens the movement of gas and fertilisers as well as oil Martin Wolf

item.title: Iran still seen capable of escalating attacks on Gulf’s energy
item.description: Analysts warn Tehran’s ability to prolong disruption to oil and gas flows ‘has a way to go’

item.title: David Zaslav, once upon a time in Hollywood
item.description: The Warner Bros boss could get a $700mn payday from the sale of his studio to Paramount

item.title: Banks prepare to offload $18bn in debt tied to EA take-private deal
item.description: Blockbuster junk bond and loan offering will test investor appetite at a time of heightened nerves over AI disruptions

item.title: Iran conflict turns shipping market into ‘wild west’
item.description: Containers are being dropped off at far-flung ports while freight rates have soared

item.title: Now it’s Paramount that faces one battle after another
item.description: Media mogul David Ellison has bagged the prize — but the deal needs to work for shareholders

item.title: Microsoft weighs legal action over $50bn Amazon-OpenAI cloud deal
item.description: Rift deepens as start-up tests limits of Microsoft’s exclusive rights to host its models

item.title: UK’s nuclear research body consults on plans to cut about 200 jobs
item.description: Move by UKNNL leads union to question government claims about ‘golden age’ for sector

item.title: Europe’s airlines eye opportunities from Iran war fallout
item.description: With services of dominant Gulf rivals disrupted, there is a brief window to provide direct flights that avoid the region’s hubs

item.title: UAE set to show leniency on tax rules for expats leaving to avoid Iran conflict
item.description: Move would be particularly important for Dubai, which has attracted wealthy individuals with its zero income tax rate Tax

item.title: Private credit’s game of footsie is getting riskier
item.description: Stronger guardrails are needed before these funds seep into the $9tn US retirement market

item.title: Texas makes its pitch to lure corporate America to ‘Y’all Street’
item.description: The Lone Star State seeks to form an economic fiefdom that harbours little patience for ‘woke’ coastal infirmities

item.title: Suspicions grow that China is exploiting FOI laws to gather UK security data
item.description: Government figures believe Beijing may be behind a significant proportion of recent requests

item.title: Trading 212 wins approval for personal pensions years after first promising them
item.description: Regulator’s nod will also allow platform to offer cryptocurrency exchange-traded notes in pensions

item.title: Investing in Nigeria
item.description: As President Tinubu visits the UK, the FT explores how his economic shock plan is playing out; Nigeria’s political age gap; moves to unblock the oil sector; the growing security crisis; investment flows; and a Q&A with the central bank governor

item.title: Investors rush in amid Nigeria’s drive to shake up bond markets
item.description: Private credit expands in attempt to bolster business investment in Africa’s most populous economy

item.title: How a vast refinery could mend Nigeria’s relationship with oil
item.description: When supply becomes boringly reliable, the economy and the national mood can shift

item.title: Move to unblock Nigeria’s oil sector hints at progress
item.description: The industry is showing signs of change but production still lags behind potential

item.title: Oil price surge will hurt US growth and fuel inflation, say economists
item.description: FT-Booth Survey shows unease about impact of the conflict on growth and prices

item.title: During the 1979 oil crisis, Canada considered gasoline rationing, new discovery shows
item.description: The stamps were only printed in limited numbers and were never put into circulation

item.title: Canadian Mike Henry to step down as BHP chief executive, will be succeeded by Brandon Craig
item.description: BHP had been working on a succession plan for some time

item.title: Telesat shares soar on hopes for defence spending by Canada and allies
item.description: Stock jumps almost 20% despite company’s lower revenue, profit loss in quarter

item.title: Israeli air strikes rock central Beirut
item.description: Iranian missile salvo on Israel kills two outside Tel Aviv

item.title: Drone company backed by Erik Prince surges 500% in Wall Street debut
item.description: Swarmer jumped to a $380mn market cap after its IPO

item.title: World’s largest miner BHP picks new chief executive
item.description: Brandon Craig to take over from Mike Henry to lead pursuit of copper growth

item.title: JPMorgan halts $5.3bn Qualtrics debt deal as AI fears chill demand
item.description: Bank along with Wall Street peers risk a high-profile ‘hung deal’ if they cannot revive transaction

item.title: Mining giant BHP names Brandon Craig as CEO to succeed Mike Henry
item.description: Henry steps down after more than six years at helm

item.title: Middle East war day 18 as it happened: Iran confirms death of top security official Ali Larijani, US counterterrorism chief quits
item.description: Donald Trump says US does not need help from Nato, Japan and Australia after traditional allies eschew war against Iran.

item.title: LNG Canada sharply boosts exports to Asia as global supplies tighten
item.description: Eight ships have departed from terminal in Kitimat, B.C., in March so far, data show

item.title: Arizona charges Kalshi with operating illegal gambling business
item.description: US state first to file criminal case alleging prediction platform offered wagers circumventing unregulated betting ban

item.title: Where do our health care dollars go?
item.description: Canada spends a disproportionate amount on end-of-life care as compared with U.S. and many European countries

item.title: Musk’s lawyers sidestepped SEC team in Twitter case settlement talks
item.description: Lawyer for billionaire Tesla founder told judge they attempted to resolve matter without Wall Street watchdog’s counsel

item.title: Lululemon reports muted growth estimates for year ahead
item.description: Activewear retailer appoints new independent board director amid calls for change from founder Chip Wilson

item.title: Nvidia is expanding its empire
item.description: The AI boom’s biggest winner moves beyond chips

item.title: CIBC boosted pay for CEO Victor Dodig in 2025 as profits jumped
item.description: Chief executives at most of Canada’s biggest banks received higher compensation last year

item.title: Will South Korea’s epic bull market survive the energy shock?
item.description: Probably, but investors should brace for more volatility

item.title: China cannot escape the energy shock
item.description: Despite renewables and reserves, it will suffer

item.title: Canadian financier Stephen Smith to buy minority stake in The Economist
item.description: Entrepreneur joins international group of wealthy families who hold stakes in the prestigious publication

item.title: Raymond James Canada names Peter Moores as new CEO
item.description: Head of company’s U.K. operations will cross the pond to replace interim CEO Paul Allison

item.description: The post Shopify’s AI-powered loans business is growing fast appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Spain warns EU against suspending carbon market to try to lower energy prices
item.description: Energy minister says suspending ETS in response to oil price surge would be a ‘big error’

item.title: Washington seeks to reassure sovereign wealth funds over tax changes
item.description: Top foreign investors have warned they could cut their US exposure if Treasury presses ahead Tax

item.title: Trapped in their starter homes: Canada has a second-time homebuyer problem
item.description: Younger people hoping to upsize for a growing family face a bigger challenge than the last generation

item.title: The Iran war is roiling commodities far beyond oil
item.description: Shortages of fuels and chemicals threaten industries from farming to pharmaceuticals

item.title: War may bring lasting change to the airline business
item.description: Western carriers will relish the chance to win some of their customers back

item.title: Does Ottawa think simply creating a new financial crime agency solves everything?
item.description: Expecting a new agency with around 150 employees to assume the mandate is impractical and naive

item.title: Labour must not lose my generation of voters
item.description: The defeat by the Greens in Greater Manchester demonstrates the danger of failing to address economic burdens Tax

item.title: Fed expected to hold rates steady as Middle East war muddies economic outlook
item.description: Central bank is likely to strike a cautious tone when it meets this week as high oil prices stoke inflation concerns

item.title: Canada’s inflation rate eased to 1.8% in February before oil prices surged
item.description: End of federal sales-tax break last year contributed to a more favourable CPI reading

item.title: Canada, U.S., Britain team up to tackle crypto scams
item.description: Law enforcement agencies launch co-ordinated effort to identify fraud victims, perpetrators

item.title: Fixing Canada’s dysfunctional mortgage system
item.description: In Canada’s mortgage system, the needs of homeowners often clash with the needs of the banks. There’s a better way

item.title: In Iran oil shock, Canada talks big but is useless to our allies
item.description: When friendly countries are clamouring for energy, resource-rich Canada can offer nothing

item.title: The UK is struggling to achieve its aims on clean energy
item.description: Our economy remains highly fossil fuel-dependent partly because we are not electrifying in the desired way Martin Wolf

item.title: Trouble is brewing among America’s corporate borrowers
item.description: Surging energy prices will add to fears of a wave of defaults
